hook.text: Across the world, forests are dying faster than they can grow back.

buildup.text: Hotter droughts stress trees, making them vulnerable to insects and disease.

discovery.text: When a tree runs out of water, air bubbles block its vessels—a process called cavitation.

twist.text: Pine beetles once kept in check by cold winters now survive year-round in warming forests.

climax.text: Western North America has lost over 100 million acres of trees to beetle outbreaks since 2000.

punchline.text: Warmer winters feed the pests that kill forests.
